pr_formatting_and_documentation.overview: Comprehensive guidelines for creating well-documented pull requests with detailed CVE research, fix rationale, and Concert API recommendations.

examples.example: 🔒 Security Fix: CVE-2023-12345 - Upgrade flask to 2.3.3
🔒 Security Fix: CVE-2024-56789 - Upgrade next to 16.0.7

rules.rule: Always start with 🔒 Security Fix emoji
Specify package and version for clarity

section.name: vulnerability_analysis
section.purpose: Provide clear understanding of the security issue

best_practices.practice: Use clear, non-technical language for impact description
Explain attack vector in terms of what an attacker could do
Include CVSS score if available from NVD
Be specific about version numbers
Clearly mark vulnerable vs patched versions
Explain any breaking changes in detail

section.name: remediation_strategy
section.purpose: Show research process and decision rationale

best_practices.practice: List sources in order of authority (vendor advisory first)
Provide specific URLs for all sources
Explain why chosen approach is best
Document why alternatives were rejected

section.name: concert_api_analysis
section.purpose: Document Concert's role in the remediation

best_practices.practice: Show Concert's specific recommendation
Document which Concert API endpoints were queried
Explain how Concert's data informed the decision
Never expose Concert API credentials

section.name: impact_assessment
section.purpose: Help reviewers understand the scope of changes

best_practices.practice: Be honest about breaking changes
Explain compatibility implications
Note if migration steps are needed

section.name: validation
section.purpose: Show due diligence in the fix process

best_practices.practice: Use checkboxes for visual clarity
Confirm no out-of-scope changes made
Note that testing will be done separately
Use full URLs, not shortened links
Verify all links are accessible
Include CWE link if applicable

section.name: next_steps
section.purpose: Set expectations for what happens after PR merge

required_fields.field: Mention of follow-up workflow if needed

best_practices.practice: Be clear this is package update only
Note that code fixes are separate workflow
Set expectations for CI/CD testing

examples.example:
fix: upgrade flask to 2.3.3 for CVE-2023-12345
- Addresses CVE-2023-12345 in flask - Upgraded from 2.0.1 to 2.3.3 - Concert recommendation: Upgrade to 2.3.3 or later - Resolves #456

fix: upgrade next to 16.0.7 for CVE-2025-55182
- Addresses CVE-2025-55182 in next - Upgraded from 16.0.6 to 16.0.7 - Concert recommendation: Critical RCE fix, upgrade immediately - Resolves #789

rules.rule: Start with "fix:" prefix for semantic versioning
Include package name and version in first line
Reference CVE ID in first line
Keep first line under 72 characters if possible

documentation_file_template.title: Create bob_task_{CVE}_{datetime}.md

checklist.item: ✅ PR title follows format with CVE ID and package version
✅ Vulnerability analysis section is complete and accurate
✅ All research sources documented with URLs
✅ Concert API analysis included with specific recommendations
✅ Changes section clearly shows old vs new versions
✅ Impact assessment addresses security and functional impact
✅ References section has all relevant links
✅ Next steps section sets clear expectations
✅ Documentation file created with research details
✅ No credentials or sensitive data exposed

pitfall.name: vague_descriptions
pitfall.description: Using generic descriptions without specific details
pitfall.example_good: "Fixed CVE-2023-12345 RCE vulnerability by upgrading flask from 2.0.1 to 2.3.3"

pitfall.name: missing_sources
pitfall.why_wrong: Makes it hard to verify the fix is correct
pitfall.correct_approach: Always include URLs to NVD, vendor advisory, and other sources

pitfall.name: no_concert_context
pitfall.description: Not mentioning Concert's role in the remediation
pitfall.correct_approach: Always document Concert API calls and recommendations

pitfall.name: unclear_impact
pitfall.description: Not explaining what the fix changes
pitfall.correct_approach: Clearly state security impact, functional impact, and compatibility

pitfall.name: missing_rationale
pitfall.description: Not explaining why this specific fix was chosen
pitfall.correct_approach: Document fix rationale and alternatives considered
